successCriteria.description: src/modules/auth/user.entity.ts exists and exports User interface matching the users table schema (id, username, passwordHash, employeeId, role).
src/modules/auth/user.repository.ts exists and exports UserRepository class with findByUsername method that queries the users table using the shared Pool.
src/modules/auth/auth.dto.ts exists and exports LoginRequest, LoginResponse interfaces and a Fastify-compatible loginSchema for input validation.

src/modules/auth/auth.service.ts exists and exports IAuthService interface and AuthService class implementing login, which validates credentials, checks employee active status, and returns a JWT token.
src/modules/auth/auth.middleware.ts exists and exports authenticateJWT and requireRole preHandler hooks; authenticateJWT verifies the token and decorates request.user; requireRole checks role.
src/modules/auth/auth.controller.ts exists and exports a Fastify plugin that registers POST /auth/login (with schema validation) and POST /auth/logout (protected by authenticateJWT).
src/modules/auth/index.ts exists and re-exports the auth plugin, AuthService, middleware, User entity, and UserRepository.
tests/unit/modules/auth/auth.service.test.ts exists and contains Jest tests for AuthService.login covering valid credentials, invalid username, inactive employee, and wrong password.
POST /auth/login with valid credentials returns a JWT token and 200 status; with invalid credentials returns 401.
POST /auth/logout requires a valid JWT and returns 200; requests without token receive 401.
authenticateJWT middleware rejects requests without a valid Bearer token with 401.
requireRole middleware rejects requests where the authenticated user lacks the required role with 403.
All inputs are validated using Fastify schema validation (loginSchema) before reaching the handler.
No sensitive data (password hash, JWT secret) is logged; error logs contain only non-sensitive information.
Login endpoint does not create an audit record because it is a read-only operation (no state change).
All async errors in the controller and service are caught and returned as proper HTTP error responses (no unhandled rejections).
tests/unit/modules/auth/auth.service.test.ts mocks bcryptjs and jsonwebtoken, does not call bcrypt.hash directly, and all tests pass.

constraints: Repository pattern: UserRepository encapsulates database access.
Audit records: Login is read-only; no audit record is created.
Input validation: Fastify schema validation (loginSchema) applied to POST /auth/login.
No sensitive data in logs: password hash and JWT secret must not be logged.
RBAC enforcement: requireRole middleware enforces role-based access on protected routes.
Error handling: All async errors in service and middleware are caught and return appropriate HTTP status codes.
Dependency direction: auth module depends on employee module; no reverse dependency.
outOfScope: Phase 4 — Employee View – Leave Application Form
Any UI components or web client code
Leave request lifecycle, balance deductions, notifications
Audit records for login (login is read-only)
